title: wolfhsm: nvm_write non-atomicity and DataLost error are not visible at call site
description: nvm_write silently performs delete-then-add with no atomicity guarantee. If the add fails after a successful delete, the original object is permanently gone and WolfHsmError::DataLost is returned. The risk is invisible from the function name. Consider renaming to nvm_replace_nonatomic, or splitting into nvm_add (create-only) and nvm_replace (overwrite with documented data-loss risk), so callers understand the hazard before writing code.
